message:
feat(indexer): add stall-detection watchdog + forensic diagnostics for #196

Adds a one-shot stall_detected NDJSON event to structured cass index runs
when no forward progress has been observed for the configured window
(CASS_INDEX_STALL_DETECT_SECS, default 120s; 0 disables). The event
carries a compact on-disk snapshot — lexical rebuild checkpoint contents
(parsed when ≤64 KiB), Tantivy segment count + bytes, and the index run
lock file — so a reporter hitting the #196 "phase:indexing, current:0"
hang does not need to chase down secondary commands to attach actionable
context to the issue.

Also emits a hint with strace/gdb/one-liner /proc/<pid>/stack snippets so
operators can grab a thread stack while the process is still live.

The watchdog is observability-only: it never cancels the run, never
touches shared indexer state, latches once per phase to avoid stderr
spam, and resets on phase transitions so "scan→index" doesn't trip it.
Unit tests cover empty, populated, and oversized-checkpoint cases for
the forensic helper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
